metadata.description: Comprehensive cook database for GMG Home Assistant integration auto-cook algorithm research.
sources: pitmaster.tools smoking calculator (comprehensive meat type library)
smoking-meat.com master chart (Jeff Phillips — Smoking Meat: The Essential Guide)
barbecuebible.com beef smoking guide and 3-2-1 rib methodology
thermoworks.com thermal guides for brisket science
KCBS competition cook standards and scoring criteria
thegrillingscience.com collagen breakdown data and temperature profiles
girlscangrill.com internal temp stages and phase analysis
behindthegrill.com time charts and brisket smoking time calculator
smokerrecipes.net smoker temperature guide for all cuts
engineercalc.net ultimate BBQ smoking hub (time & temp data)
miniwebtool.com meat smoking calculator (wood pairings + timing)
Oregon State University fish smoking safety guidelines
Jeffrey Steingarten / Serious Eats reverse sear methodology

_schema.meat_type: canonical meat type key from metadata.meat_types_covered
_schema.cut_name: human-readable cut name including method variant
_schema.weight_lbs_min: minimum expected weight in pounds for this cut
_schema.weight_lbs_max: maximum expected weight in pounds for this cut
_schema.smoker_temp_f_maintain: target maintained smoker/grill temp °F (may differ from start for ramp methods)
_schema.estimated_time_hours_min: minimum estimated total cook time in hours
_schema.estimated_time_hours_max: maximum estimated total cook time in hours
_schema.time_per_lb_minutes_min: min minutes per pound estimate for this method
_schema.time_per_lb_minutes_max: max minutes per pound estimate for this method
_schema.usda_safe_temp_f: USDA minimum safe temp °F for reference
_schema.stall_expected: true if evaporative cooling stall is expected at this temp/method
_schema.stall_start_temp_f: internal temp where stall begins (null if no stall)
_schema.stall_end_temp_f: internal temp where stall ends (null if no stall)
_schema.stall_duration_hours_min/max: expected stall duration range in hours
_schema.wrap_recommended: true if wrapping is used for this method variant
_schema.rest_time_hours_max: maximum recommended rest time in hours

phases.name: phase name (warmup, bark_and_smoke_ring, stall, collagen_render, wrap_and_stall_push, cook_through, rest, etc.)
phases.internal_temp_range_f_start/end: meat internal temp at phase start/end (null for time-based phases)
phases.smoker_action: what to do with the smoker/grill during this phase
phases.description: what is happening chemically/physically to the meat

wood_pairings: recommended wood types for this meat
_schema.notes: additional context or tips specific to this method variant

phases.name: bark_and_smoke_ring
phases.description: bark forms, smoke ring develops at 130-140F

phases.name: stall
phases.smoker_action: hold steady — do not increase temp

phases.name: collagen_render
phases.smoker_action: check probe tenderness at pull temp
phases.description: collagen converts to gelatin (160-205F), meat becomes tender

phases.name: rest
phases.smoker_action: wrap and rest in cooler or warm place

cooks.notes: unwrapped cook — maximum bark and smoke ring.

phases.name: paper_wrap_stall_push
phases.description: paper preserves bark while pushing through stall

cooks.notes: butcher paper wrap — preserves bark while pushing through stall.

phases.name: wrap_and_stall_push
phases.description: eliminates evaporative cooling, pushes through stall

phases.name: collagen_render
phases.description: collagen converts to gelatin in sealed moist environment

cooks.notes: foil wrap (Texas crutch) — faster stall push, softer bark.
